examples: In stands dominated by trees such as aspen, however, incipient gypsy moth outbreaks are quickly suppressed by viral epidemics.
Heavy commitment by an executive to a course of action, especially if it has worked well in the past, makes it likely to miss signs of incipient trouble or misinterpret them when they do appear.
Just like the earlier famine, this incipient one is man-made.
I, the incipient novelist, even as a boy, was fascinated by the way people do talk.
That changed with the formation of Mercosur, an incipient customs union also involving Paraguay and Uruguay.
